messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "som-window-cumulative-misread",
 "title": "The 1% Year-1 anchor is a 36-month number wearing a 12-month label",
 "narrative": "obtainableFactor's 1% rests on Prospeo's staged benchmark, read as an annual snapshot. But Prospeo itself frames SOM as revenue captured over 1-3 years, IdeaPlan says 'one to three years', Wise says 'three to five years', and TryBuildCo's own worked example lands at 0.1% of SAM — below the model's stated floor of 0.5%. The cross-check comparable (Tractian '$1.2M first year') is unverified against its own source, which shows $200K starting revenue. For a pre-launch industrial entrant with zero installed base and no certifications on day 0, the honest 12-month number is the bottom of the reference class, not its midpoint. The whole YAM figure is a period-mismatch error, not a market estimate.",
 "category": "fact",
 "targetNodes": [
  "obtainableFactor"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "Every source underlying the 1% benchmark defines the obtainable percentage as a cumulative 1-3 or 3-5 year capture window, not a Year-1 snapshot — the model pins a multi-year ratio to a 12-month YAM",
   "refs": [
    "digest:obtainable-benchmark#c1",
    "digest:obtainable-benchmark#c5",
    "digest:obtainable-benchmark#c6"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "The two cross-checks that were supposed to independently confirm 1% both fail on inspection: TryBuildCo's worked example yields ~0.1% of SAM (below the model's 0.5% floor), and Tractian's revenue started at $200K — the '$1.2M first year' number cited in the derivation does not appear in the source",
   "refs": [
    "digest:obtainable-benchmark#c7",
    "digest:obtainable-benchmark#c10"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "obtainableFactor 0.01 overstates the honest pre-launch Year-1 capture by roughly 3x; the corrected value sits near 0.3% of SAM, and the sensitivity band's low edge (0.5%) does not even contain the reference-class outcomes",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:obtainableFactor",
    "profile:Stage"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "The node is marked 'triangulated' and the refinement cycle already 'caught' one benchmark misread (Year-2 read as Year-1), so the team believes the period question is settled. The surface appearance of a completed correction hides that the corrected source itself defines the percentage over a multi-year window — the fix repeated the same category of error one level down.",
 "expectedObviousness": "medium",
 "falsifier": "A documented comparable — an industrial hardware entrant into DC infrastructure — that captured ≥1% of a regional SAM in its first 12 months from commercial launch, with dated revenue evidence.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.55,
  "rationale": "The window-semantics mismatch is directly visible in the cited sources' own text (Prospeo, IdeaPlan, Wise all describe multi-year windows), and the Tractian cross-check number is unverifiable against its source — this is closer to a documented error than a speculative risk.",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "obtainableFactor",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.003,
   "note": "Multi-year benchmark converted to an honest 12-month figure for a zero-installed-base entrant, consistent with TryBuildCo's 0.1% worked example and Tractian's actual $200K start"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Quote-to-PO conversion time in the first CE opportunities",
   "where": "Own CRM pipeline reviewed at month 6; cross-referenced against TED (Tenders Electronic Daily) award timelines for rack-power line items",
   "threshold": "Median cycle >9 months at month 6 implies Year-1 closes are structurally impossible at the 1% level",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Published first-year revenue disclosures of comparable DC-hardware entrants (Asian PDU makers entering EU)",
   "where": "Company registries / Crunchbase / vendor press releases announcing first EU revenue milestones",
   "threshold": "No comparable showing >0.5% regional SAM capture in year 1",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Build a bottom-up pipeline model (named CE accounts × realistic win rate × cycle time × rack capacity) to replace the top-down benchmark before committing the Year-1 plan",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "obtainableFactor",
   "note": "The ledger's own promotionPath names this; doing it pre-launch converts a period-mismatched heuristic into a defensible number"
  },
  {
   "action": "Re-denominate Year-1 targets in design-wins and framework qualifications rather than revenue, and set the revenue plan on a 36-month SOM curve",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Aligns internal expectations and investor communication with what the reference class actually delivers in 12 months"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "base-rate-analogy",
 "riskId": "risk.base-rate-analogy.som-window-cumulative-misread"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

The cross-check comparable (Tractian '$1.2M first year') is unverified against its own source, which shows $200K starting revenue. doing it pre-launch converts a period-mismatched heuristic into a defensible number"},{"action":"Re-denominate Year-1 targets in design-wins and framework qualifications rather than revenue, and set the revenue plan on a 36-month SOM curve","type":"strategic","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Aligns internal expectations and investor communication with what the reference class actually delivers in 12 months"}]}
